$comment: Gaffer model-routing REGISTRY (audit item I1). The router (bin/route-model.mjs) reads this to turn a routing context into a concrete model id. ADDING A MODEL/PROVIDER OR CHANGING A TIER IS A CONFIG EDIT HERE — never a code change (acceptance criterion). Override the whole file with GAFFER_MODEL_REGISTRY=<path>, or override any single resolved model with GAFFER_PLAN_MODEL / GAFFER_IMPL_MODEL (those always win, preserving today's behaviour).

tiers.$comment: Named tiers → concrete model ids. A tier is the unit a phase/rule routes to; the router only ever emits a tier's model id. Add a provider-specific model by adding a tier (e.g. "local": "ollama:qwen2.5-coder") and pointing a phase or the escalation order at it — no code change.

phaseDefaults.$comment: Per-PHASE default tier. The cheapest-correct layer that can do the phase wins; failure-escalation + risk/complexity rules move off this default at routing time. decompose/plan reason deeply (strong); implement/test default mid; self-review mid; merge-conflict-resolve is delicate (strong); onboarding scans cheap.

localSeam.$comment: SEAM for local models on the cheap idle/scan path (privacy + ~zero cost). Set "enabled": true and point "tier" at a tier whose model id is a local provider (e.g. add tiers.local = "ollama:qwen2.5-coder" and set tier: "local"). When enabled, the idle/scan phases ("onboarding") resolve to this tier. FULL local-provider wiring (a provider adapter that turns "ollama:..." into the right CLI flags / base URL) is a documented FOLLOW-UP — this only wires the registry seam so the routing decision is already config-expressible.
